title: docs: e2e-tests: ci: Add new OIDC e2e tests with Dex + Oauth tutorial
author: illume

findings.description: push trigger has no paths filter, so every push to main/rc-*/testing-rc-* (including unrelated changes) triggers a full Dex go build + Minikube startup + Playwright run (~10-15 min). The pull_request trigger correctly uses paths; the same filter should be added to the push trigger.

No test.setTimeout() override. Default Playwright timeout (30s) may be insufficient for tests waiting on Minikube pod readiness (kubectl wait can take 2-3 min). Consider test.setTimeout(120_000) or a beforeAll readiness wait.
README shows 'dex version' as a sanity check but the Dex binary built from source does not support a 'version' subcommand in v2.45 (exits non-zero). Use 'dex --version' or 'which dex' instead.
Pidfile-based process management validates ps -p PID -o args= against a specific command pattern before signalling, preventing accidental SIGTERM to recycled PIDs.
CSRF-state tampering rejection test (test #14) verifies forged OAuth2 callback is rejected with 4xx and no session cookie — a meaningful security regression guard.
New tutorial architecture diagram (ASCII + prose) accurately explains the OAuth2-Proxy → Dex → Headlamp flow with screen reader alt text.
Port-forward readiness check uses three-step verification (process alive, PID matches, port accepting TCP) before declaring stack ready, avoiding fragile sleep-based waits.
